message:
refactor(gmail): migrate HTTP layer to GoogleRestExecutor and add
discovery-driven integration tests

Complete the Gmail connector's migration to the shared Google API
substrate by replacing the hand-rolled reqwest retry loop with the
shared GoogleRestExecutor from fcp-google-discovery:

client.rs:
- Replace raw reqwest::Client with GoogleRestExecutor wrapper
- Consolidate get/get_with_params/post_json into a unified
  execute_with_retry() method using GoogleExecuteRequest
- Restore GmailClient::new(token) convenience constructor
- Add explicit Default Accept: application/json header
- Use GoogleResponseMode/GoogleResponseBody for response decoding
- Implement custom Debug to redact auth tokens

connector.rs:
- Add OAuth refresh flow via load_default_google_provisioning_bundle()
- Scope validation now differentiates between OAuthRefresh (can check
  granted scopes), CredentialReference (deferred), and direct
  BearerToken (cannot introspect post-configure)
- Health status derives from auth variant, not separate auth_mode enum
- Add resolve_gmail_required_scopes() and parse_string_array_field()
  helpers for robust scope/config parsing
- Replace auth_mode.label()/summary() calls with
  auth_label_for_materialized() and map_auth_error()

integration.rs:
- Add shared_generation_overlap_exposes_current_gmail_baseline_drift
  test that compares discovery-generated artifacts against the
  handwritten introspection surface and manifest.toml
- Add mock OAuth token endpoint returns token_type + expires_in
- Add generated_approval_name/manifest_approval_name helpers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
